description_long: This object is a large, wedge-shaped block of cheese with a pale yellow-tan color and numerous round holes of varying sizes scattered across its surfaces, reminiscent of Swiss or Emmental cheese. The block has a slightly irregular, almost trapezoidal form, with one end tapering to a narrower edge and the opposite end being broader and more rectangular. The surface is matte and smooth, with the holes giving it a distinctive, porous appearance. There is no visible rind or outer coating, and the cheese appears to be solid throughout. The object is unwrapped and shows no branding or packaging. Its size and shape suggest it is an oversized, stylized representation of a classic cheese block, likely intended as a playful or decorative urban object.
description: A large, wedge-shaped block of pale yellow cheese with many round holes and a smooth, matte surface. The cheese is unwrapped and has no visible rind or branding.
description_view_0: The cheese is seen from its narrow, pointed end, highlighting the depth and vertical alignment of the holes along the side.
description_view_1: The broad, rectangular face of the cheese is visible, showing a flat surface with scattered holes of various sizes.
description_view_2: The opposite narrow end is shown, with a tall, flat face and several prominent holes, emphasizing the cheese's thickness.
description_view_3: The back, slightly curved face of the cheese is visible, with a similar distribution of holes and a smooth, unbroken surface.
